Okay, so one thing we added, so I had Vince, and Vince, I'm gonna show you guys his actual full cartoon, so he hands sketched a cartoon for me. I got it Christmas Eve, it was like my best Christmas present ever, it was awesome. But one of the things is we're taking, and I'm gonna talk about this tomorrow as well, is like, our goal is, ClickFunnels is cool, but you have to understand what a funnel is for it, for you to want to buy it, right? Same funnel hacks, like you know what a funnel is, and like, what, like, it doesn't make sense. And so you'll see the video tomorrow, I'll show you the events made for me where it's a hands sketch video that explains like what a website is, and kind of does that. Now last, maybe before the last webinar, Vince emailed me, like, here's all the screenshots of your presentation, or from the video he made me, I'm like, oh sweet, so I threw him in here, and now I lead the webinar that way, just because, I can't do how many people when I start the webinar, it's like, what's the funnel, I can't figure out that piece, I'm like, let me just get that out of the way before we even get started, like, hey, before we get started, this is what a funnel is, and this is kind of a fun way to explain. So I put his earning disclaimer thing in here, my giveaway, okay, I need the cool stuff. Okay, so we have three secrets here. Okay, now, one thing we'll talk about too is like, one of, a lot of you guys' tendencies when you're teaching your content, is you're going to want, you're going to want to teach too much of the content, like you notice I'm not teaching people, I'm not showing, like, step, here's how you do the whole thing, I'm trying to get them to understand the concept, right? So for me the concept I'm trying to get to understand is your goal is to just model a funnel, right? And so I'm sharing stuff that's very much more like strategic, and not so much like, like Chad's walked out, but for example when he was doing his stuff, it was all like, as a doctor we do this, and we do this, and all this stuff, and it's like, that's not what people need or they want, they don't want to know all this stuff, they just need to have that idea, that concept of what it is, and be excited by it. So I show all this stuff just to get them excited by the concept, and show explanations, and showing just in a very, very step by step format. The more you can do that, where things now become very, very tangible, remember I talked about earlier like prolific and specific, this is very, very specific. It's not me saying, okay, you got a model, funnel, it works, it's like, let me show you how I did it. So I'm gonna look at what this one, look at what theirs look like, here's what this look like, so I'm being as specific as possible, because I don't want them to try to get what I'm saying, I just want to make it, like you were saying earlier, like first grade level, like this is how it works, you know. Are we at testimonial there? This is where I talked about, remember like I added this back in after someone was like, I have no idea, if I only had a supplement I would do it, so this is where I added that back in, and I started going out to the core market, so obviously coaching is a big market we hit, so I wanted to cover that. Info products are a big market, we hit e-commerce is a big market, offline business is a bigger market, so I use this to kind of bridge this gap between all the different people that I know are gonna be on the webinar, because a big problem we have a lot of times, especially when you start doing Facebook stuff that Michael talked about tomorrow, like you're gonna be targeting different segments and bringing them in, and for example Liz is like, she targets women, right? But let's say Mike started targeting men and getting how much men on the webinar, like men are gonna show up and like they're not gonna relate because the webinar is talking more towards women, so if she knew that like there's gonna be people coming from different groups, figure out what are those groups gonna be and tie that into your presentation, does that make sense? Cause I know right now I'm not driving a lot of like real business traffic, but I'm going to be, and so I wanna make sure that my presentation has that so when a chiropractor comes on here he's like, oh that's how this fits for me, or when a dentist or when a financial planner, you know? I'm gonna show you examples of all my stuff, this is a question I got a ton, we're just like, cause I talk about how much money different funnels are making, how do you know how much money funnels are making, how do you know how much I'm like, I gotta figure out how to illustrate that, so I showed that and actually show myself valuing about what a funnel looks like, walk through the process. I try to make, this slide's one of my favorites, when we first launched ClickFunnels we made this really cool promo video and I was trying to make our product like an absolute, like not a necessary, but something you have to have and so when I did this I figured like what's one thing that I know you have to have to be successful in business, right? So it's an email autoresponder and I think most people would agree with that, so that's a known, like a known thing, so I get everyone to agree and I say okay, there's two things you have to be successful, without it you cannot be successful, the first is an email autoresponder, you guys agree with that? I was like yeah, like that's true, you can't be successful at email autoresponder, so they disagreed to point number one, so subconsciously they've agreed with everything I've just said, so the next thing I say, like they already, they automatically have to say yes because they said yes here, right? So I'm like and the second thing you have to have to be successful is ClickFunnels, so in their head they're like wow, like I have to have both those things or I can't be successful, okay? So I anchored a known, a known thing, a concept that they all agree with already to what my product is, that makes sense? So it's kind of some NLP ninja stuff, but that's kind of the concept of this slide which is really, really powerful, is taking a known thing that they are, a known belief they already have and then tying my product into that, and that's how I kind of did it here. So that slide's a fun one for me. I show the whole process here. Okay, let me get to the close because some of the fun stuff starts happening. And what was cool for me when I did at GKSC, which is I think why it works so good, I got at this point, I said, look, the first 50 people, and I stopped, and there's already like four or five people in the back of the room, and I said, in fact, you guys should go look, look, there's four people already back there, we haven't gotten to this yet, so now it's the first 46 people in the back of the room, this is what we're going to do, and all of a sudden, everyone starts popping back up, and they're sprinting back, and like, I didn't have a chance to even get past here, because people are like, I don't know what it is, but I want that, and I want to make sure I'm the first 50, and so it causes this huge table rush, I'm in the middle of my presentation, which was awesome. because it's so many confusion of like, how do I sign up, and then here, boom, I pushed them here, typically I have a countdown clock here, it broke, are my power points, it starts at 30 minutes, they count some 30 minutes down to zero, I used to have the bottom left-hand corner there, and the first time we did it, we had a 10 minute countdown clock, and sales are coming, sales are coming, as soon as the clock hits zero, sales completely stopped, just like dead, and I was like, what in the world, so I told my brother, I need a 30 minute clock, so I had to go build a 30 minute clock, we plug that in, and then what happens is the entire 30 minute sales are coming, and sales are coming, and sales are coming, and sales are coming, and so if you do a countdown clock, make sure it's not short, because as soon as it hits zero, people are done buying, like that's just, in their head, I missed the offer, and it's gone, so we make it long enough, 30 minutes is about how long it takes me to start going through all of these questions, if you want to see how I did it, it was kind of a nightmare, but I basically went through all of our frequently asked questions that weren't answered in the thing, and this is what it looks like on PowerPoint, because it's just, I have a question where exit, enter, exit, there's a nightmare to create, that's what it looks like, and then when I go to it, excuse me, looks like that, and then I start clicking through, and it shows each one, one at a time, enter, exit, that's the animation, so that's how I kind of did that, and this is what I just added recently, and it's been working really, really good, it's cool because it keeps me focused on the questions I want them to ask me, in the order I want them to ask me, and everything kind of builds on each other, and so that's kind of why I kind of did it, and I can see my question that way, I can plug in once, I know people are asking, and it gives me the ability to really have good control, I have any issues, just go here, just go, so giving them, if they get stuck and out, otherwise people get frustrated and they throw their hands up, because we'll see the people that are emailing support right now, I mean we ended up, what do we say, 23,000? Yeah, 23,000, my guess is they'll probably get another five or six thousand that come through in the next two or three hours, just from support, following the people and getting them to get their credit cards to work on, kind of thing, so if you don't have that, you lose out on a ton of sales that way, the PayPal financing option, do you guys hear me talk about that a little bit, in the follow-up sequence, so I talk about it here a little bit, but what happens is in like three days from now or four days from now, they'll get emailed, they go into a lot of detail, about like, hey, how would you like first to finance it, and I'll show you guys the follow-up sequence tomorrow, actually, but we get insane amounts of sales through the PayPal buy it now, or people can't buy it, whatever, PayPal finances it form, no interest, no monthly payments for six whole months, and you guys get the money immediately, which is really, really cool. The cons of it are, they have to be in the US, they have to have, or they have to have good credit, but the nice thing is they have no interest, no payments for six months, what happens is they go to PayPal, PayPal goes and like, deans their credit, if they're good, PayPal will give them a credit line of like five or ten, I think up to ten thousand dollars, and then it'll go buy your product, and then you, again, you get the cash immediately, and then for six months, they don't have to do anything, and six months from now, they get billed, and they, if they want to refund, they can't, because it's past clickbait, or not clickbait, PayPal, PayPal's refund policy and everything, okay, so even people that have, I was thinking about you, with your physical business, like, in chat, like, you guys can finance all like, like actual people coming to your clinic through PayPal as well, I know some people are doing that, where they're financing the whole thing, it's come in, it's like, because a lot of times you think about, especially offline businesses, like, I can't sell, how do I sell a three thousand dollar package if they don't have insurance, it's hard, right, but if you come with PayPal financing, they get it for free, you get the money, you go through the whole thing, and six months, now they got to deal with PayPal, and they finance it that way, so it's kind of, there's a lot of really cool things with PayPal financing, so you can plug that into your thing, again, plugging it as an email sequence happens three or four days later, you'll pick up another 25% of sales just from that, it's really pretty like, pretty much like clockwork, another 25% or more, people who will buy through PayPal financing, and I will, in the future, I'm going to have, I'll talk more about it in the presentation about PayPal, but the ordering system's not, Todd's got something weird set up, so they have to email support to get it right now, but, and that's kind of the gist, so I want to kind of go through some of those CSC saws, some of the core pieces that I want to make sure were important, any questions about that at all, or any of the pieces of it, how many of you guys were on the, were on the W reading webinar with Howard Berg? Okay, so this is like an example of a paid one we did with Howard Berg, where it's basically, it's a paid webinar, he got on for four hours, and so it's free, and then we build people $100 five days later, if they stuck on, or they could pre-pay $80, and so those ones, I'm not, for us, we weren't trying to sell on at all, Howard's gone for four hours, taught people how to read, and it worked awesome, it did great, but it's just kind of a different thing, if I'm doing the perfect webinar, my whole goal is I want as many people as possible, because I want to just close this, I don't want any barriers of payment, or anything, I just want everyone on, and yeah, I recorded the automated version on Friday, so we will be coming out next week, we're going to launch our automated version, there's pros and cons, when you guys, you'll find, when you do an automated version, your conversion rate will drop, but the cool thing is that, because you're not involved with it, like that webinar will be doing webinars every hour, on the hour, for someone in the world, 24 hours a day, seven days a week, and so conversion drops, but your actual money goes up, because it's just happening way more often, and you don't have to do it all the time, so there's kind of pros and cons, so yeah, we will be rolling out an automated, I'm going to keep doing it live every Thursday, but then have the automated one as well, because we have a lot of affiliates who want an automated one, and then I'm gone a lot of weeks, so I don't, I want to be able to plug that automated one in when I'm, when I'm not here, I said, I think I said help guarantee your success, I want to help guarantee it, no it's hard, there's, yeah, technically, like to be really technical, you can get in trouble for any, almost 90% of stuff we all say in webinars, you get in trouble for, so you kind of pick your battles, which ones you're okay with, which ones you're not, we, if you want, we could have a discussion outside about like, FTC and stuff, I don't want to put on camera, because I don't want to get in trouble for what I say, but what we found from like, people that have worked with FTC and things like that, and all those kind of issues, typically the way the process works if you get trouble, it starts with a BBB, do your BBB complaints unresolved, it goes from there to Attorney General, from Attorney General to FTC, everyone, every FTC lawyer we ever talked to said if your BBB is clean, they're never going to investigate you, if it's dirty, then they start digging really, really deep into things, so I try to make things as clean as I can, but there's things you have to do to be able to sell, and then you kind of pick your battles, and then you just make sure that you refund anyone that asks, you keep your BBB clean, you sell good products, things like that, and then you don't get on people's radars, you don't get any, at least we haven't, we never had any issues at all, like the speed as you're moving through the presentation, I don't know, my bulletin is subconscious things, but no, when I get to that, when I get to the closed stuff, I do feel like I'm picking a pace each time, and I'm trying to do that, part of it is because I'm getting tired, I want to keep getting things through, but part of it is because I remember when, along with Bill Glazer's public speaking stuff, I remember that he was talking about how, because he speaks very, very slowly, he's like, when he got on stage, he had to learn to speak really, really fast, because the faster you speak, it's harder for people to catch everything, and that makes them more likely to want to invest, because they're like, like this stuff sounds really good, but it's hard to keep up, and so if you go really, really slow, people are like, oh, I get it all, so for me, I get more and more rapid, partly because I'm excited, but partly because the energy and the momentum, all that kind of stuff, but then also it's like, the faster you talk for whatever reason, even they don't understand everything, like, they're getting more, you know, I don't know how it works, but that's kind of what I found, so, but I don't know if there's like a real reason that I've done it outside of just, it just happens, but don't be afraid to talk fast, that's a lot of fear people have, that you shouldn't worry too much about, no, no, like I said earlier today, like when I first started, I was so shy, and I was nervous, and I'd go through, and I'd just be like, hey, this is Russell, came from 10 years of my life pitching stuff, and so that's what I said, the first time it's gonna be hard, that's why I'm trying to show you guys stuff so that you can emulate, like I wish someone would show me from day one, that like, just standing up or sitting different on my desk will change like my voice, you know, talking at this level comes off normal, or this level comes off dead, you know, and stuff like that, so, but yeah, it's a process that you'll learn, and you'll get better and better at every single time you do, which is why it's important, I mean, listen, do you feel like your first, well, I would love to watch, like, do you have them all recorded, like your first versus now, I've been so much different from like your tonality and your energy, and like all those pieces that, not so much that you were like studying and trying to make it better, but you just, you get more confident, and you get more excited, and you start seeing things, and you just, it changes, right? That was the name of the file. That was awesome, very cool. All right, so this is the game plan, guys, we are at 512, I want to bring Natasha up and talk to her about, so she's, I want to bring up for a couple reasons, one is that she's been very successful doing webinars, and also she's taken this, and used this process and the skill set to also put on live events like this, and sell people at events, and I'll have her kind of tell some of these stories, but she's an amazing person, and how many of you guys saw her video when you registered, when you signed up for our program? I will never do anything other than the stack. Every opportunity that I can stack, I totally will. I love it. I'll even do it in sales letters and stuff. I don't even know if you're supposed to do that, but they work so well. I just don't want to mess with it. So I was trying to think how it worked well with us on webinars. So Rich and I were talking about our $5,000 offer. First of all, I was terrified to make a $5,000 offer. And it was a really small intimate group. I remember, I feel bad now, like, because I came the day before she was doing the clothes and it says 25, 30 people there. I mean, he's made millions. Maybe we should just do an application. I was like, fear for me. I don't even, I would never dare to do that. But anyway, I was like, I got the board done. It's back from Kinko's. We are selling this thing and I am making sales. So I was like totally nervous. So I was like, oh my gosh, Russell Brunson wouldn't do what I was about to do. But yeah, so what we did was we got this idea, I have to say, from my dad at our rehearsal dinner. He had come up with the top 10 things that Rich needs to know before he marries me. And so he had this huge board. And he had put like, like, silver tape over each one. And he would like, rip it off and unveil it and then like, tell what, you know, the 10, 9, 8 all the way down to 1. So I'm going to guess it's probably Rich's idea. Was it your idea, baby? Probably said to do the board. And so we get up, we start talking about our offer. And first of all, we started seeding in the Facebook group beforehand. So they were already kind of like excited and like wanted to buy something else. But so we had this huge board up there with like the silver tape over it. And so I was like, all right, so component number one, and I was like, rip it off. And we would tell what the thing was and give the value. And then the third thing, like we did one after another, and it was like this reveal. And people were coming up to us afterwards, they were like, that was such an awesome sale. Like we couldn't wait, we wanted to know what else was next. Like you were talking about one thing, but like, we were excited about that, but we wanted to know what it was. So I actually transferred that to our PowerPoint slides. So I actually have black boxes over the things. And so, and so, and it works. And they, they got excited again. Well, question is, Heather, because she's on now on webinars, she's done live events. And again, it's the same process we're all going through you guys. I mean, if you all haven't heard this story before, like, so I heard Russell talk about trial closes at a vet and I was just mesmerized. I was like, oh my gosh, I have to do trial closes, but I couldn't remember them all. And so like I wrote them on like post-it notes and like, it's so excited to use them for the webinar. I had them like all over my windows. So my desk is like, you know, right up against the windows. So I have them like all over like the shades, all over on my side of my desk and everything. And so I would like test them out, whatever I like looked at, I would do that trial close. And it worked really well. I mean, I think we made probably like, I think eight grand or something on my first webinar that I did with trial closes. Yeah, he's like, what is that on your shoe? I was like, yeah. And the crazy thing about these trial closes, one of my like go-tos is, are you getting this? Does this make sense? And actually, one of my coaching clients told me that they just thought it was just so kind of me to always say, does this make sense? Because it sounds like I really want to make sure that they're getting it. And I do want to make sure I'm not just saying it, although I now realize that I probably look at the chat box way too much. But yeah, so getting used to them, you'll feel like completely silly going through the trial closes. But they work. I've done them live. I've done them on webinars. I do it to lawyers. It's kind of fun. It's like, try to call it until I get what I want. So yeah. You can't say no after that. Yeah. So I mean, I'm telling you, I haven't done the perfect webinar yet, but I have done like lots of different elements of it. And so even if you don't do it perfectly, you can do a half-baked webinar with just some of these elements that are in it, and you can still get good results. So that's cool. I love it. Well, questions you guys have for? I really can't. I mean, I really can't. I mean, definitely the Q&A, what I've done is I always wait till the end, generally. Anyways, because if you stop, someone's going to ask some really stupid question, and then it's going to throw off your whole thing. I had this one lady when I was going back to the webinar report. This was, I think, like last Thursday or something. And she's like, I can't get any sound. I can't get any sound. Can you help me? I mean, like, thank goodness I didn't look at that in the chat box. Because I mean, what can I do at that point? I really... She can't hear you anyway. Yeah. Yeah. No. No. Yeah. So like, no need to stress yourself out over that. There's just no need. One thing I just think about real quick with the trial closes was interesting when you look at it, there's like, when I say, when I say, you guys get in this, you're thinking I'm talking about, like, are you getting this concept, right? But your subconscious mind is thinking, am I getting this product? Like, if I show like click funnels, I'm like, are you guys getting this? And you're like, yeah. Your mind just said, yeah, I'm getting click funnels. And it was kind of interesting, like, there's multiple layers of these trial closes of what you're saying to someone where they get it, you get in the concept, but their subconscious mind is picking up, are you getting the product? You're getting this anyway. It's really cool. We gave away the farm because it was our first 5k offer. And sometimes when you do your first bigger ticket item, you are like, I got to make sure that people want to buy it. So we had two VIP days here in Boise with me and Rich. The short answer is that when we got started, I navigated towards blogging, like I love blogging. And so I started doing that and SEO and everything. But my husband, Rich, who was an ad exec for 14 years, was, you know, gravitated towards PPC. So he does all of our paid advertising. So I mean, paid advertising is really how we've built our list in a big way. So and I know nothing about it. That's why I'm lucky to be married to someone that knows a lot about it. So I am on Facebook pretty much every day, monitoring, liking, I mean, people get excited when I like their stuff, which is really, I mean, it was kind of weird for me at first when someone was like, someone actually wrote me a message and said, Hey, Natasha, I just want to thank you for liking my stuff. And then what was the second question? So not that much surprisingly, actually, I'll let the group go in. And this is where I allow people to flex their leadership muscles. And I let them answer. My group is pretty trained to know that, you know, if you want coaching with me, you got to pay. So it's not, it's not a bunch of free Facebook coaching. So it's really about setting expectations. That's really the huge part of that. The group knows we do not spam, like we've had a couple of people and they immediately like my leaders in the community immediately are like, did you see so and so? Can we remove their post or whatever? So, you know, setting expectations up that this is a help and be helped. Don't just be the mooch. Give value back. It's an opportunity for them, like I said, to flex their leadership muscle. So a lot of it is the clients that are in our higher level programs. Like I specifically tell them in their small group coaching sessions with me, I'm like, you all are the leaders of the community. You guys are like so much like sisters you don't even know. You're the monkeys, the ranchers. Yeah, so they know like so and so is in charge of the ranch while I'm out of town or whatever. So, but you just, and you can hand select people and just say, hey, you know, I would love for you to help me build this community and just get those people in place and let those people kind of be your your seed folks in there. If you ask a group, people will fight over it. Like when we do it like, hey, who wants to be moderate and we'll get like insane. Everyone wants to be like, oh, I want to be part of like I'm always on here anyway. And so as soon as you get an initial group, if you just ask, like they'll they'll beg. Yeah, people are really craving like leadership roles in a way to like really prove themselves to you and into themselves. I mean, I, you know, I have a client that said, you know, I really want to find another way to help out in the community more because I really just, it makes me feel good to be a leader in the in the community. And so I'm just giving them those opportunities through go to meeting. And I do, well, that's what I've moved away from one on one. I am such a huge fan of group coaching. I was really scared to move to group coaching. Because I kept thinking, well, gosh, are people going to get as much value out of it? And I found that people love it because they learn from one another. And so, you know, we do it through go to webinar, and I record it, I allow people to send questions in. I tell them they need to send their questions in beforehand. So I have an idea of what we're going to be talking about. And, and even if they can't attend, I'll still answer their question on the call and I record it and send it out. So, and yeah, we have clients all over the world. So at this event, I was so, I'm so excited. We're having our first international folks that are coming to our event this summer, one's coming from Russia, and the other one's coming from London. So that's a cool milestone when someone's willing to fly across the pond for you. So we're excited to Boise, Idaho. If there has ever been a weakness, it is that we think we can do everything. So that's our theme for 2015 is to get more of a team, because we really do, we have, we learned how to do so much stuff. And I'm a little bit of a control freak. So that's my big challenge for the year is really learning how to effectively outsource and build a team. So proof to be said that you can make a lot of money, just, you know, you don't have to have this like enormous team that probably benefit us tremendously. And as we're moving to the next level in our business, we certainly want to bring on a team, but it's not completely necessary. So there's your click funnels, you just need click funnels. Yeah, you just need click funnels and an auto responder. That's it. And Russell, just add Russell, you need to be here every single month. So the group coaching that I've done, I think the most that we've had is probably 15. And, and as we're about to launch a new funnel, and we're going to have a lot more. And so yeah, we are going to try to divide it up. Probably out. I think that if I had 20 people, that would be the most because not all 20 are going to show up. You know, and, and not everyone has questions every week. And so it's, you know, I find that it's, you know, not that big of a problem for me at this point. And another thing is with our small group coaching, I had like a coaching club where they would get like one month, a group coaching one hour or whatever. But for my small group coaching at my higher level, I say we go on those calls until we get everyone's questions answered. And they love it. I mean, it's never really lasted more than an hour and a half or I think two hours is the max that it's ever been in like 24 months. But it's the value is huge because they understand like, okay, I can come in, I can get like literally whatever question I need to have answered, answered in that call. So I hope that answers your question. Sure, I'll give you, well, I'll give you, how about I give you our new one that we're working through? Because we've had stuff kind of all over, all over from like a dollar trial to $5,000 or 7, 7,500. So we are starting off first with our free template, like, so it's called the perfect client template, it's like a fill in the blank number, where you learn how to create your ideal client, because that's what a lot of people are missing. And then as you go up, seven bucks is the is called the perfect client. And it's just, I think it's a 28 minute training. And it just goes deeper into the ideal client, like why the template works and you know, how you can use it just, you know, just a little, just a little bit more than the free template. Then we're actually going up to something called the celebrity brand blueprint. And that is, so my whole process is like a seven part process that I take all of my clients through, we've been doing it for several years, of how they're going to define their brand and start to teach them about a marketing funnel, just to give them a roadmap. So this is like nine, you know, 10, 15 minute modules. So I'm not giving them too much, because that was my biggest flaw, who was giving them way too much information. I mean, I learned that the hard way. In fact, I had a really bad webinar that did, I sold it, people got so much value, but it was horrible for upselling my next program, because I gave so much information, it was painful. So I was like, no wonder no one wanted to buy that thing, because I gave too much information. So anyways, at this point, then they can pay $100 application fee, or to get a strategy session. And from that point, we'll either sell them into $9.97 or $39.97 for my branded Kickstarter program, which is my much more pared down version of my process. It used to be like 30 or 40 hour training. And I realized that that was just way too much. So now it's six, one hour modules. And that's it. And that they want the home study course is $9.97. And it comes with worksheets and all that. And then if they want the group coaching with me, it's $39.97. And then we'll offer a $9.97 offer for VIP days. With us, it'd be, play two VIP days with us here, but that's going to be like super selective. And we've had two people come through this year that like, they're like, they're probably like, Hey, so we got this, this 100 grand, and two guys like $100,000 thing. Like really like, Yep, we want it like, all right. So I'm glad we had like made up this fake thing that then we could come back and like, actually make it because there's some people that like just buy it a premium, you know, like, when I go to the gas station, there's like the cheap gas, the middle gas and expensive gas. And I don't know the difference, but I know that I'm scared if I put the cheap gas in my car is going to die. Like, so I buy the most expensive just because I'm scared, right? It's the same thing. Like, there's just something about price points where if you give people three options, there's a percentage people always want the most expensive. So if you don't have it there, then things make up like, here's my $50,000 thing, and then someone's somewhere that's going to be the right fit, you know, right. Does that make sense? And that's probably what I'd feel more comfortable with, at least at this point. But you know. One thing I want to stress too is that I know that, you know, when the most years get initially involved, like you go through some training talks about value ladder, and I think I like how people think about it, because it's important to know like kind of where you're going, but also as a detriment sometimes, because sometimes you guys try to build everything before. And like, the key is like, you got to make a $5,000 program and sell it and get people in there before you make your 10. And then, you know, so like knowing kind of this is where I want to go, but don't, and you're not building all those things, you're focusing on just like, what's the next piece? You go do that with Liz, three or four times by the thing where she's like, okay, I'm right for the next thing. I'm like, let's just keep, you got to see like, keep selling more people. Like, there's going to be a time where that audience is big enough, where now it makes sense to do the next high ticket thing or to go do a continuity or whatever the next thing is. But like, focusing on one thing, building the audience up before you release like the next thing. And so, it's good to know kind of where you're going, but also like just picking one and just focusing on that, getting people in, building your audience, and then you can figure out what logically makes the next best one thing that you said, just reminding me, I'd love to share with you all. So, so many people feel like they have to like, create every, every component of their program before they get started. And the thing that I just sold in September or October, did not create like, there was nothing, there was nothing before I sold it. But we collected, you know, $35,000. Of course, we're going to deliver on it. But, you know, I started, I sold our branded Kickstarter program, trying to have a beta test this past summer. And got a ton of people in there, hadn't even created it yet. Like, I had the welcome module up, because I wanted them to have a back office. But they understood that this was a beta test price. And so, they, they understood that they were getting a special deal because it wasn't done yet. For my branded professional course that I sold. And on a webinar, $8,000 that we made, the course wasn't created. I had one module in there. Because like, honestly, if the thing bombed and like, no one, and I misread what people wanted, like, I didn't want to have put like, months and months and months of hard work into it. And so, you know, just keep that in mind. Don't get so bogged down and spending like, four months trying to create this gorgeous membership site with all of these like, 50 hours of training, only to find out that like, that's not exactly the right thing to be offering in the first place. I agree a million percent. We done over a million dollars in funnel hacks sells before I did the first training.
